title: Ptilostemon dyricola
taxonomy: Ptilostemon dyricola is classified within the family Cistaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the rockrose family. This family is part of the larger order Malvales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as the mallow and hibiscus families. The genus Ptilostemon is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region and are characterized by their thorny stems and yellow flowers. Ptilostemon dyricola is a specific species within this genus and is endemic to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.

intro: Ptilostemon dyricola, also known as Dyman's Globeflower, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising treatment for various conditions, including wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of Ptilostemon dyricola include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
